data.title: Bursa Opens Slightly Lower As Markets Digest BNM’s Steady OPR
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened slightly lower on Friday, with the FBM KLCI down 2.20 points, or 0.13%, at 1,714.94 by 9.02 am.

Market sentiment remains cautious as investors digest Bank Negara Malaysia’s (BNM) decision yesterday to maintain the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) at 2.75%, reflecting a steady monetary stance amid moderate inflation and resilient economic growth.

Regional cues were mixed this morning.

While Singapore’s Straits Times Index rose 0.36% to 4,845.49, other Asian markets were consolidating ahead of US economic data later in the day.

The cautious tone on Bursa was mirrored by a slightly stronger ringgit against the US dollar, following BNM’s signal that the current OPR level is appropriate to support domestic growth while maintaining price stability.

BNM highlighted that Malaysia’s economy continues to benefit from resilient domestic demand, higher tourist spending and strong electrical and electronics exports, while headline and core inflation remain moderate.

The central bank flagged potential global risks, including tariffs, geopolitical tensions and market volatility, but maintained that supportive fiscal and monetary policies underpin the economic outlook for 2026.

Trading volume this morning was led by NatGate, CapitalA and Velesto, with gainers including Kobay, KLCC and Panamy, while Nestle and PPB were among the top decliners.

Investor focus remains on earnings updates, global economic cues and the impact of stable interest rates on corporate financing costs.

As markets navigate a balance between growth optimism and external uncertainties, Bursa’s opening reflects measured sentiment ahead of a potentially active trading session.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher, FBM KLCI Up 0.42%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher on Thursday, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) rising 7.09 points, or 0.42%, to 1,712.90 at 9.10 am.

The broader market also showed gains, with the FBM70 up 87.58 points (+0.50%) to 17,540.19, the F4GBM climbing 4.36 points (+0.43%) to 1,015.94 and the FBMEMAS advancing 52.01 points (+0.41%) to 12,616.95.

The FBMSHA rose 46.74 points (+0.38%) to 12,290.23.

On the active board, SMRT led trading with 35.24 million shares at RM0.210, up 2 sen.

Debutant Guan Huat Seng Holdings (GHS), which recently made its ACE Market debut, was actively traded at RM0.240, down 1 sen, with 12.22 million shares changing hands.

Other active counters included Velesto at RM0.290 and IJM at RM2.570.

Top gainers in early trading were Nestle (+0.60 sen), Allianz (+0.36 sen), and United Plantations (+0.30 sen), while Mercury (-0.055 sen), Zhulian (-0.05 sen) and Hextech (-0.04 sen) led the losers.

Market sentiment at the opening appeared cautious but generally positive, as investors assessed early trading movements.

data.title: Singapore Stocks Open Higher, STI Up 0.36%
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange (SGX) opened higher on Friday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) rising 17.17 points, or 0.36%, to 4,845.49 as of 9.10 am.

Market breadth was positive, with 149 advancing stocks versus 63 decliners, on a total trading volume of 143.22 million shares valued at S$219.33 million.

Banking counters were mixed in early trade, with DBS down 0.24% at S$57.94, UOB at S$38.38, and OCBC at S$20.75.

Key blue chips including Keppel and Singtel traded at S$10.98 and S$4.45 respectively, while CapitaLand Investment was at S$3.05.

On the derivatives front, MSCI Singapore Index Futures were at 454.85, while Nikkei 225 and FTSE China A50 futures were at 53,810.00 and 14,978.00 respectively, signalling cautious optimism in regional markets.

Investors are tracking early market sentiment following global cues, with Singapore’s benchmark index maintaining a steady start to the day.

data.title: Bitcoin Hovers At US$90,000 As Ethereum Retreats
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market entered a consolidation phase on Jan 23 as Bitcoin hovered around the US$90,000 mark and Ethereum retreated toward key support levels, reflecting a cautious tone across digital assets.

Bitcoin remained range-bound between US$88,000 and US$92,000, with traders citing indecisive flows amid mixed macro signals. The US$90,000 threshold has emerged as a psychological pivot for market participants, with accumulation seen on dips but limited conviction for a breakout.

Meanwhile, Ethereum weakened toward the US$2,900-US$3,000 band, underperforming Bitcoin as investors rotated defensively into higher-liquidity assets. The move reflects dampened risk appetite, with analysts flagging that a sustained breach below US$2,900 could trigger further technical selling.

Market sentiment has been shaped by a muted backdrop in global risk assets, with equities edging higher ahead of key central bank signals while geopolitical and regulatory developments continue to cloud visibility for crypto-linked funds. Derivatives activity remains steady, suggesting institutional traders are hedging rather than unwinding exposure.

Traders are now watching two inflection levels: US$92,000 for Bitcoin, which could indicate upside momentum if breached, and US$2,900 for Ethereum, viewed as a near-term support that could determine broader altcoin direction.

For now, price action suggests consolidation with a mild bearish tilt, as crypto markets take cues from macro catalysts and await clarity before committing to the next directional leg.

data.title: Ringgit Maintains Stability As Dollar Shows Slight Strength
data.description:
The Malaysian Ringgit (MYR/RM) showed relative stability against key currencies in the first three weeks of January 2026, according to Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) data.

As of the time of writing, the Ringgit traded at 4.0455 against the US dollar (USD), showing a slight depreciation from 4.0580 on Jan 2.

The local unit also eased marginally against the euro (EUR) to 4.7280 from 4.7708 at the start of the month. Against the British pound (GBP), the Ringgit stood at 5.4327, down from 5.4708.

Regional currency comparisons were mixed.

The Ringgit gained against the Indonesian rupiah (IDR100), trading at 0.0239 today compared with 0.0243 on Jan 2, while it remained steady versus the Singapore dollar (SGD) at around 3.15.

The Ringgit also strengthened against the Thai baht (THB100) to 12.9270 from 12.8255, and showed modest gains against the Japanese yen (JPY100) at 2.5564 compared with 2.5919.

BNM’s data reflect the Ringgit’s resilience amid global currency fluctuations, although it continues to face pressures from a stronger US dollar and regional market volatility.

Trade flows, commodity prices and external interest rate movements will remain key drivers for the Ringgit’s trajectory in the coming weeks.

data.title: SGX Opens Higher, STI Gains 22.97 Points On Broad-Based Buying
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange (SGX) opened higher on Thursday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) rising 22.97 points, or 0.48%, to 4,832.85 as of 9.09 am.

Market sentiment was broadly positive, with advancers outnumbering decliners 158 to 52 on the day, and a total trading volume of 314.25 million shares worth S$192.07 million.

Key gainers included UOB, which rose 1.52% to S$37.33, and OCBC Bank, up modestly to S$20.51.

DBS and Singtel were also in positive territory, reflecting early confidence in financial and telecom sectors. Among small caps, MarcoPolo Marine traded at S$0.163, while YZJ Shipbuilding was at S$3.46.

Other indices tracking sectoral performance were mixed. The iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index stood at 1,483.97, while the iEdge S-REIT Index was at 1,126.60.

Regional and thematic indices such as the iEdge-OCBC Singapore Low Carbon Select 40 Capped Index and iEdge Southeast Asia+ TECH Index also saw modest gains.

Derivatives trading showed 36,000 contracts exchanged, with futures contracts including MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 452.95 and FTSE China A50 Index Futures at 15,031.00.

Early moves in currency futures reflected USD strength, with USD/SGD futures at 1.2808 and USD/CNH at 6.9372.

The market opened cautiously optimistic, tracking gains in regional peers as investors digest global economic developments and earnings updates.

data.title: Global Chip Stocks Rally On AI Optimism As Huang’s Remarks Boost Sentiment
data.description:
Global semiconductor stocks climbed on Thursday as comments by Nvidia’s Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang at the World Economic Forum in Davos reinforced investor enthusiasm for the artificial intelligence (AI) sector.

The rally reflected growing confidence that the demand for AI hardware and chips remains robust, underpinning strength across global markets.

Huang’s remarks at Davos helped support the broader AI trade, contributing to gains in memory and logic chip stocks as investors weighed the long-term potential of AI technologies to fuel demand for processing power and infrastructure.

In related market activity, Nvidia invested US$150 million in AI startup Baseten, part of a US$300 million funding round, signalling a strategic shift to focus on inference workloads within AI, a segment analysts expect to grow substantially in coming years.

NVIDIA’s shares rose around 2.9% on the news, rebounding from prior weakness in broader technology stocks.

At Davos, top technology executives sounded bullish about the AI sector’s future despite geopolitical uncertainties. Huang dismissed concerns of an AI bubble, noting strong underlying demand for GPUs and related compute resources, with cloud and enterprise usage expanding rapidly.

BlackRock CEO Larry Fink also weighed in at the forum, rejecting the notion that the AI boom constitutes a speculative bubble.

He positioned the surge in AI investment within a broader competitive landscape between major economies, emphasising the need for collaboration and wider diffusion of AI technologies to sustain growth.

Supporting the optimism in chip stocks, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) reported better‑than‑expected fourth‑quarter results, boosting its shares by over 6% to record highs.

Other major semiconductor names, including Nvidia, AMD, ASML and Micron, also saw notable gains, underscoring strong investor appetite for AI‑linked hardware demand.

Agencies

data.title: Bursa Midday Gains Driven By SMRT And Nestle As Market Eyes Stability
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended gains at midday, with the FBM KLCI rising 7.98 points or 0.47% to 1,713.79, led by broad-based buying across key indices.

The FBM70 climbed 91.66 points or 0.53% to 17,544.27, while the FBM Emas index added 56.94 points or 0.45% to 12,621.88.

Market activity showed strength in small and mid-cap stocks, with SMRT leading trading volume at 80.1 million shares, closing 1.5 sen higher at RM0.205.

Other actively traded counters included PMETAL (RM7.42, +2 sen), ZETRIX (RM0.82, +0.5 sen), IJM (RM2.62, +13 sen) and TANCO (RM1.30, +1 sen).

Among top gainers, Nestle surged RM2.60 to RM118.20, Allianz rose 60 sen to RM22.18, Carlsberg added 36 sen to RM17.72, UTD Plantations gained 36 sen to RM30.66 and Heineken climbed 26 sen to RM22.80.

On the losing side, F&N eased 16 sen to RM36.00, Keihin fell 10 sen to RM1.20, KGB lost 10 sen to RM5.37, Petronas Gas slipped 8 sen to RM18.72 and Silvervest retreated 8 sen to RM2.82.

Investors remained focused on blue-chip stability amid cautious optimism, with sentiment supported by selective buying in defensive and consumer-related stocks.

The overall market breadth pointed to a modestly positive mood, while trading volumes suggested measured participation as investors assess domestic and global economic cues.

data.title: Wall Street Snaps Back On Greenland Deal, Bank Earnings Boost
data.description:
Wall Street closed higher on Wednesday, snapping a sharp selloff from the previous session, after news that the US had reached a framework agreement related to Greenland, easing concerns over fresh tariffs on European allies.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 588.64 points or 1.21% to end at 49,077.23, while the S&P 500 gained 78.76 points or 1.16% to 6,875.62, marking its biggest one-day percentage gain in two months.

The Nasdaq Composite advanced 270.50 points or 1.18% to 23,224.83.

Markets were buoyed after US President Donald Trump said a framework for a future deal on Greenland and the broader Arctic region had been reached, adding that previously planned tariffs on European countries scheduled for Feb 1 would not be imposed.

The rebound followed Tuesday’s broad selloff, the worst daily performance for all three benchmarks since October, as investors reacted positively to the avoidance of a potential escalation in trade tensions.

Analysts said the announcement allowed markets to refocus on economic fundamentals, particularly corporate earnings.

Banking stocks were among the strongest performers, with the regional banking index jumping 4.7% to its highest close since November 2024, supported by upbeat earnings from several lenders.

Citizens Financial Group surged 7.1% to a record high after posting a 31.7% jump in quarterly profit, while Truist Financial rose 1.8% on higher interest income and investment banking fees.

All S&P 500 sectors ended the session in positive territory, led by energy stocks. Halliburton climbed 4.1% after beating earnings estimates, while EQT Corp and Expand Energy advanced 6.5% and 4.5% respectively as natural gas prices rose on cold weather.

Airline stocks also gained after United Airlines rose 2.2% on a stronger-than-expected outlook for the first quarter and the full year. Delta Air Lines, American Airlines and Southwest Airlines added between 1.1% and 2.4%.

Netflix, however, fell 2.2% after issuing a muted outlook and announcing a pause in share buybacks to fund the acquisition of Warner Bros Discovery’s studio and streaming businesses.

Market participants said the rally reflected relief that tariff threats had been rolled back, reducing uncertainty at a time when investors remain sensitive to geopolitical risks and policy shifts.

Despite the strong rebound, analysts cautioned that volatility could persist as markets continue to track developments in global trade relations, earnings momentum and policy signals from Washington.

Reuters

data.title: KLCI Closes At Fresh 5-Year High On OPR Boost, Cooler Geopolitics
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended on a strong note on Jan 22 as investors cheered the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) status quo and easing geopolitical tensions, sending the benchmark FBM KLCI to its highest level since 2019.

The index surpassed its previous peak of 1,713.45 from Feb 27, 2019, marking a key psychological breakout.

At the close, the FBM KLCI rose 11.33 points, or 0.66%, to 1,717.14, extending the 1,705.81 finish recorded on Jan 21.

Broader participation also firmed, with the FBM 70 up 0.75% to 17,582.74, the FBM EMAS higher by 0.67% to 12,649.35 and the FBM Shariah gaining 0.59% to 12,315.25. The F4GBM index, tracking ESG-aligned constituents, climbed 0.68% to 1,018.43.

As for the most active stocks for the trading session, Velesto Energy Bhd topped the volume charts, up 2.5 sen to 31.5 sen on heavy trade of over 131.3 million shares, while SMRT Holdings Bhd added one sen to 20 sen with nearly 97 million shares changing hands. Nationgate Holdings Bhd surged 19 sen to RM1.19, while Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd firmed 10 sen to RM7.50. Zetrix AI Bhd bucked the trend, easing 0.5 sen to 81 sen.

With sentiment turning constructive and liquidity flowing into mid- to large-cap names, traders expect follow-through interest into the coming sessions barring external shocks.

data.title: Market Breadth Turns Firm As Nestle Leads Top Gainers
data.description:
The stock market closed broadly higher on Jan 22, with market breadth firmly positive as 622 gainers outpaced 435 losers, while 589 counters were unchanged, 1,066 were untraded and nine were suspended.

Blue-chip defensives and consumer names helped lift sentiment, with Nestle (M) Bhd topping the gainers’ list, surging RM1.40 to close at RM117. Allianz Malaysia Bhd added RM1.08 to RM22.66, followed by brewery counters Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd gaining 44 sen to RM17.80 and Heineken Malaysia Bhd rising 36 sen to RM22.90. Plantation major United Plantations Bhd climbed 42 sen to RM30.72, rounding up the top gainers for the trading session.

On the losing side, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd led decliners, slipping 16 sen to RM36 on 1.05 million shares. Atlan Holdings Bhd declining 13 sen to RM2.72, Petronas Gas Bhd down 10 sen to RM18.70, Kein Hing International Bhd dropped nine sen to RM1.21 and Solarvest Holdings Bhd also eased nine sen to RM2.81, rounding out the top five losers.

Meanwhile, newly ACE Market debutant Guan Huat Seng Holdings Bhd fell half-a-sen to 24.5 sen to close its debut day with high trading volume of more than 31.2 million shares.

Overall, trading remained rotational, with investors selectively positioning in defensives, financials and consumer-related counters amid a broadly constructive tone.

data.title: Wall Street Rises For Second Day As Tariff Relief And Strong Data Lift Sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street ended higher on Thursday, marking a second straight session of gains as investors were buoyed by tariff relief from US President Donald Trump and upbeat US economic data.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 306.78 points or 0.63% to 49,384.01, while the S&P 500 advanced 37.73 points or 0.55% to 6,913.35. The Nasdaq Composite climbed 211.20 points or 0.91% to 23,436.02.

The gains followed Trump’s decision to rescind tariff threats against European allies, a move that eased market jitters after earlier volatility linked to trade tensions over Greenland. The rally came a day after the S&P 500 recorded its biggest daily percentage gain in two months.

“It’s very weird to wake up every day as a money manager and you do not know whether it is Christmas morning or Friday the 13th,” said Gregg Abella, CEO at Investment Partners Asset Management.

Despite the recent rebound, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite remain down about 0.4% for the week, while the Dow is broadly flat, after losses earlier in the week triggered by renewed tariff concerns.

Risk appetite was also reflected in small-cap stocks, with the Russell 2000 rising 0.8% to a record closing high. All seven of the so-called Magnificent Seven technology stocks ended higher, led by Meta Platforms, which jumped 5.7%, and Tesla, which gained 4.2%.

Corporate earnings continued to drive individual stock moves. Procter & Gamble rose 2.6% after reporting quarterly results, while Abbott slid 10%, its biggest one-day percentage drop since 2002, after forecasting current-quarter profit below expectations. GE Aerospace fell 7.4% despite projecting annual profit above estimates, and McCormick dropped 8.1% after flagging weaker profit prospects in 2026 due to higher costs.

Economic data also supported sentiment. US consumer spending rose solidly in November and October, pointing to sustained economic momentum, while initial jobless claims increased less than expected. Separate data showed the US economy grew by a slightly stronger-than-expected 4.4% in the third quarter of 2025.

Trading volume on US exchanges totalled 18.30 billion shares, compared with a 20-day average of 16.91 billion shares, indicating heightened market activity as investors repositioned amid shifting policy signals and earnings developments.

Reuters

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Ahead Of BNM Policy Decision
data.description:
The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar on Thursday, as investors priced in expectations that Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) would keep the overnight policy rate (OPR) unchanged at 2.75% during its policy meeting later in the day.

At 8 am, the local currency strengthened to 4.0350/0570 versus the greenback, up from Wednesday’s close of 4.0445/0495.

The firmer tone in the US dollar was largely offset by market anticipation of BNM’s decision, which is now drawing the focus of traders and investors, as reported by Bernama.

The ringgit also gained against a basket of major currencies in early trade. It rose against the Japanese yen to 2.5462/5603 from 2.5598/5631, firmed against the British pound to 5.4142/4437 from 5.4237/4304, and strengthened versus the euro to 4.7101/7357 from 4.7337/7395.

Against regional peers, the local currency was broadly higher. It appreciated versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1389/1565 from 3.1502/1543, against the Indonesian rupiah to 238.1/239.7 from 238.7/239.3, versus the Thai baht to 12.8831/9612 from 13.0086/0301, and against the Philippine peso to 6.80/6.85 from 6.82/6.83.

Market attention remains firmly on BNM’s Monetary Policy Committee decision later today.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Jan 22, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on Jan 22, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: HSIF Rebounds From Intraday Low, Uptrend Remains Intact
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) maintained its long position call on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), citing easing bearish momentum and a resilient bullish structure.

The research house said HSIF rose 108 points on Wednesday to close at 26,570 points after opening at 26,460. The index initially slipped to an intraday low of 26,301 before rebounding to a high of 26,719, forming a bullish candlestick. During the evening session, HSIF added a further 46 points to last trade at 26,616.

RHB Research noted that selling pressure has moderated, with support emerging at the 20-day simple moving average. The relative strength index has also started to trend higher, signalling an improvement in bullish momentum. The index remains above both its 20- and 50-day SMA lines, indicating that the broader uptrend remains intact.

“As long as the index stays above the 25,000-point support, the current retracement should be viewed as a pullback within an ongoing uptrend,” the research house said, adding that it continues to maintain a positive trading bias.

RHB Research reiterated its recommendation for traders to stay on the long position initiated at 21,416 points, with a stop-loss set at 25,000 points to manage downside risks.

The initial support level is pegged at 25,600 points, followed by 25,000 points, while immediate resistance is seen at 27,400 points, with a further upside target at 28,000 points.

data.title: HSIF Extends Gains As RHB Maintains Long Position Call
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its positive trading bias on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) after the index extended its gains in the latest session.

The HSIF added 76 points to settle at 26,646 points, after opening at 26,573 points. The contract rallied to an intraday high of 26,796 points before briefly dipping to a low of 26,483 points, eventually closing with a bullish candlestick.

During evening trading, the index continued its advance, rising a further 271 points to last trade at 26,917 points.

RHB Research noted that the HSIF remains firmly above both the 20-day and 50-day simple moving average lines, indicating that bullish control remains intact.

The research house added that the relative strength index continues to hold above the 50% level, signalling that bullish momentum is picking up.

If momentum strengthens further, RHB Research said the index could climb towards the next resistance level at 27,400 points, following a rebound from the 20-day SMA line.

For now, the research house continues to maintain a positive trading bias on the HSIF.

RHB Research reiterated its recommendation for traders to stay on long positions initiated at 21,416 points, recorded at the close on Apr 14, 2025. To manage trading risks, the stop-loss level has been fixed at 25,000 points.

In terms of technical levels, initial support is seen at 25,600 points, followed by 25,000 points, while immediate resistance is marked at 27,400 points, with the next resistance level at 28,000 points.

data.title: Stock Picks: Unisem And Farm Fresh
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) flagged Unisem (M) Bhd and Farm Fresh Bhd as technical stock picks, citing improving bullish momentum and potential upside from recent price action.

Unisem may extend its bullish trajectory after staging an upside breakout, RHB Research said.

The stock is trading above its 21-day simple moving average and has closed above the RM3.45 resistance level, signalling a bullish setup.

The research house said further upside could lift the counter towards the next resistance at RM3.60, followed by RM3.70.

However, a fall below the RM3.35 support level would negate the bullish view and signal a resumption of the correction phase.

Farm Fresh is also showing signs of further upside after testing its immediate resistance with strong trading volume.

RHB Research noted that the stock formed a long lower shadow in the previous session, indicating strengthening bullish momentum.

A decisive move above the RM2.85 resistance could see the counter advance towards RM3.00 and subsequently RM3.15.

Conversely, a decline below the RM2.70 support level could weaken sentiment and trigger a renewed correction.

data.title: Kospi Smashes 5,000 Mark For First Time
data.description:
South Korea’s Kospi index blasted past the 5,000-point level for the first time on record, surging more than 2% in early trade on Jan 22.

The milestone came hours after US President Donald Trump said he had reached a “framework” for a deal on Greenland, triggering a wave of buying across Asian markets.

The Kospi’s breakout set the tone regionally, with Japan’s Nikkei climbing roughly 1% at the open as investors reacted to the latest geopolitical twist.

Market analysts said traders appear to be positioning for potential diplomatic and economic spillovers from the announcement, though full details of the purported framework remain unknown.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Dips At Midday As Heavyweights See Mild Profit-Taking
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its morning losses at midday as investors engaged in profit-taking on selected heavyweight stocks.

By 12:30pm, the FBM KLCI had slipped 1.36 points, or 0.08%, to 1,715.78 from yesterday’s close of 1,717.14. Among major indices, the FBM 70 rose 16.26 points to 17,599.00, while the FBM EMAS slipped 3.96 points to 12,645.39. The FBM SHA posted a modest gain of 7.95 points to 12,323.20. The F4GBM, meanwhile, also declined 0.66 points to 1,017.77.

The overall market breadth, meanwhile, turned negative with 476 decliners versus 453 gainers. Some 548 counters remained unchanged, 1,253 were untraded and 21 suspended. Total turnover hit 1.77 billion shares worth RM1.47 billion.

High-volume movers from the morning trading session were Zatrix AI Bhd at 81.5 sen with trading volume of 64.6 million shares, followed by Capital A Bhd at 54.5 sen after gaining 2.5 sen with 54.1 million shares changing hand and Tanco Holdings Bhd losing two sen to trade at RM1.29 with more than 40 million shares traded. Other active stocks include Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd at RM7.51 after marginal increase of one sen with 35.4 million shares and Velesto Energy Bhd at 30.5 sen with 33.8 million shares.

Nestle (M) Bhd continues to lead the decliners as the counter dropped RM2 to RM115, followed by United Plantations Bhd easing 30 sen to RM30.42 and Hong Leong Bank Bhd falling 22 sen to RM23.98. On the flipside, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd (F&N), Hong Leong Industries Bhd and Perusahaan Sadur Timah Malaysia (Perstima) Bhd attracted buying interest, reflecting steady demand for selective consumer and industrial stocks. F&N and Hong Leong Industries added 20 sen to close their midday trading at RM35.84 and RM17.18, respectively, while Perstima gained 18 sen to RM1.98.

Overall, the mixed performance highlights a cautious market tone, with investors prioritising liquidity and trimming positions in high-profile stocks while monitoring activity in mid- and small-cap counters.

Analysts say the pullback reflects routine profit-taking rather than a broad sentiment shift, with investors keeping a close eye on heavyweight and high-volume counters for signals in the afternoon session.

data.title: Stock Picks: JCY International And Malayan Cement
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified JCY International Bhd and Malayan Cement Bhd as near-term stock picks based on improving technical indicators and bullish price setups.

JCY International’s uptrend may resume if the stock forms an interim base near its recent breakout point.

RHB Research noted that the counter is trading above the RM0.39 resistance level, signalling that bullish momentum remains intact.

Following a period of consolidation, the research house expects upside movement to resume, with the stock potentially advancing towards RM0.41 and subsequently RM0.43.

However, a drop below the RM0.37 support level would invalidate the positive outlook and trigger a correction phase.

Meanwhile, Malayan Cement is testing its breakout point while maintaining a bullish technical structure.

The stock recently closed above the RM7.80 resistance level on a bullish candlestick, indicating that upward momentum remains in play.

RHB Research highlighted that the 21-day simple moving average continues to trend upwards, reinforcing the short-term bullish bias.

If momentum strengthens further, the counter could climb towards RM8.20, followed by RM8.60. A decline below RM7.40 would negate the bullish setup and signal the start of a correction.
